queue.goal_command: /goal Build a live-billing evidence packet for the $500 budget without mutating providers
success_criteria: Owner-system exports or explicit no-spend confirmations are attached for each live-proof backlog line.
The strict intake validator passes for the filled proof packet.
The budget verifier still shows Codex OAuth/Pro excluded and OpenAI API target $0.
blocked_claims: Does not claim live billing complete until owner-system proof is accepted and reconciled.
Does not claim Operational Launch Ready.
queue.why_goal_is_appropriate: This is the top blocker for completing the budget objective without weakening truth/provenance boundaries.

queue.goal_command: /goal Produce the Exact-Site Hosted Review first-send approval packet with no sends
success_criteria: Recipient evidence, send copy, target list, and approval asks are packaged without sending.
Hosted-review outcome proof source is ready for future allocator scoring.
Human approval requirements are explicit before any outreach.
blocked_claims: No customer traction or hosted-session fulfillment claimed.
queue.why_goal_is_appropriate: Exact-site hosted review is the primary wedge that can justify future budget reallocations once proof exists.

queue.goal_command: /goal Build the one-city launch proof packet under a $10 paid-test ceiling
success_criteria: City blockers, targets, proof artifacts, and optional paid-test request are packaged.
Any paid-test proposal is capped at $10 and requires approval.
No activation or ad launch happens in the packet.
blocked_claims: No active coverage or customer traction claim.
queue.why_goal_is_appropriate: A bounded city packet connects budget allocation to launch learning while preserving approval gates.

queue.goal_command: /goal Harden support_triage cost/cadence proof from cache and no-change suppression
success_criteria: Support triage cost/cache proof is current and separated from live billing proof.
No-change suppression is explicit for repeated support-triage runs.
Flash-first or deterministic routine paths are preserved.
blocked_claims: No broad automation quality claim from fixture proof.
queue.why_goal_is_appropriate: Support triage is a P0 canary lane and needs recurring cost discipline inside the compressed Paperclip envelope.

queue.goal_command: /goal Run a Public Launch Ready conversion audit for the world-model buyer route
success_criteria: Public Launch Ready copy remains polished and conversion-oriented.
Unsupported operational claims are blocked or scoped precisely.
Buyer route findings are tied back to world-model-product-first doctrine.
blocked_claims: No customer, payment, rights, hosted-session fulfillment, or Operational Launch Ready claim.
No broad not-launched/apology language added only because live ops are gated.
No live production smoke or provider mutation.
queue.why_goal_is_appropriate: The public route can improve conversion while the budget and live proof systems remain human-gated.
